bin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Edo.

bis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Bislama.
bise: noun. Alternative form of bice (blue pigment)
bless: verb. To make something holy by religious rite, sanctify.
blin: verb. To cease (from); to stop; to desist, to let up.
bye: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pouye.
byline: noun. A line at the head of a newspaper or magazine article carrying the writer 's name.
bys: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Burak.
ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
ess: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Siberian Yupik.
inbye: adjective. Inside the house; inside an inner room of the house.
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
isle: noun. An island, usually very small.
lei: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemio.
lenis: adjective. Relatively weakly articulated, hence unaspirated and possibly voiced; especially as compared to the others of a group of
lens: noun. An object, usually made of glass, that focuses or defocuses the light that passes through it.
ley: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lemolang.
lib: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likum.
libs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of lib
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
line: noun. A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
liney: adjective. Resembling or characterised by lines.
